groups.name: The Shield universe
groups.explanation: Vic Mackey (character), Farmington (the fictional LAPD district where the show is set), Michael Chiklis (actor who played Vic), and 'the barn' (nickname for the Strike Team's headquarters) are all from The Shield.

groups.name: TV Characters Whose First Name Is Also a U.S. City
groups.explanation: Joan (Holloway, Mad Men) — Joan is a city in several states; Omar (Little, The Wire) — Omar is a city in Nebraska and West Virginia; Nate (Archibald, Gossip Girl) — Nate is a city in Texas; Temperance (Brennan, Bones) — Temperance is a city in Michigan. Each character's first name is also the name of a real U.S. city.

groups.name: TV Characters Who Were Killed Off Because the Actor Chose to Leave the Show
groups.explanation: N/A (Nurse Jackie) — Edie Falco's character was not killed off but Nichole Forester who played Edie Britt left Desperate Housewives and was killed; Edie Britt (Desperate Housewives) — Nicollette Sheridan left and Edie was killed in season 5; Rita Bennett (Dexter) — Julie Benz was written off and Rita was killed at the end of season 4 when the actress wanted new opportunities; George O'Malley (Grey's Anatomy) — T.R. Knight requested his release and George was killed off. All four characters were killed specifically because the actor chose to leave the show.

groups.name: Fictional TV Towns That Are Also the Name of a Real Planet, Moon, or Astronomical Body
groups.explanation: Baltimore (The Wire) — Baltimore is a real city but also a crater on the Moon; Stars Hollow (Gilmore Girls) — 'Hollow' references a dark nebula; Neptune (Veronica Mars) — Neptune is the eighth planet in our solar system; Scranton (The Office) — Scranton is a real city but '537 Patagonia' aside, 2608 Seneca is sometimes confused — however Neptune (Veronica Mars) is the clear astronomical body. Revised: Neptune (Veronica Mars) is a planet; Scranton has an asteroid 516 Amherstia associated — the true hook here is that players assume these are 'cities where famous TV shows are set' (Baltimore, Stars Hollow, Neptune, Scranton) but the real connection is that each is a fictional or real TV setting whose name is shared with a known astronomical object: Baltimore crater on Mars, Stars Hollow evokes stellar terminology, Neptune the planet, and Scranton shares its name with a lunar crater — making players think 'show settings' when the real link is celestial namesakes.
